chapter_title.ar: باب الرَّجُلِ يُقْتَلُ وَ لَهُ وَلِيَّانِ أَوْ أَكْثَرُ فَيَعْفُو أَحَدُهُمْ أَوْ يَقْبَلُ الدِّيَةَ وَ بَعْضٌ يُرِيدُ الْقَتْلَ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 46 – The man is killed and for him are two guardians, or more, so one of them forgives or accepts the wergild, and one wants the (retaliatory) killing

text: قَضَى أَمِيرُ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ ( عليه السلام ) فِيمَنْ عَفَا مِنْ ذِي سَهْمٍ فَإِنَّ عَفْوَهُ جَائِزٌ وَ قَضَى فِي أَرْبَعَةِ إِخْوَةٍ عَفَا أَحَدُهُمْ قَالَ يُعْطَى بَقِيَّتُهُمُ الدِّيَةَ وَ يُرْفَعُ عَنْهُمْ بِحِصَّةِ الَّذِي عَفَا .

en.hubeali: Ali Bin Ibrahim, from his father, from Ibn Fazzal, from Yunus Bin Yaqoub, from Abu Maryam,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Ja’far<sup>asws</sup> having said: ‘Amir Al-Momineen<sup>asws</sup> judged regarding the one with a share who forgave, so his forgiveness is allowed; and he<sup>asws</sup> judged regarding four brothers, one of them forgave, he<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘He would be given the wergild, and from the remaining ones, (the wergild) would be raised from them by the share of the one who forgave’.
